h2h_summary: Ghana and Panama have no prior head-to-head competitive history; their 2026 World Cup Group L match on June 17 will be the first-ever meeting between the two nations.
key_players_away: Anibal Godoy (captain, defensive midfielder, San Diego FC)
team_style_home: Ghana under Carlos Queiroz deploy a defensive-minded 4-2-3-1 with Thomas Partey anchoring a double pivot, prioritizing compact organisation and rapid counter-attacks through the pace of Semenyo and Inaki Williams on the flanks.
team_style_away: Panama under Thomas Christiansen set up in a disciplined 4-2-3-1 or 5-4-1, pressing high with intensity (leading CONCACAF qualifiers in high turnovers and pressed sequences), maintaining a compact defensive block and relying on quick transitions rather than possession-based play.
wc_history_home: Ghana have appeared at four World Cups (2006, 2010, 2014, 2022), with their finest hour coming in 2010 when they reached the quarter-finals in South Africa before losing to Uruguay on penalties, becoming only the third African nation to achieve that feat.
wc_history_away: Panama made their World Cup debut in 2018 in Russia but were eliminated in the group stage with three defeats (including a 6-1 loss to England), scoring only once through Felipe Baloy's historic first goal for the nation; they did not qualify for 2022.
group_context: Group L features England (heavy favourites), Croatia (2018 finalists), Ghana, and Panama — with Ghana and Panama facing each other in the opening round on June 17, making this match a virtual must-win for both sides to remain in contention for a top-two finish and progression to the Round of 32.
strengths_home: Explosive wide attackers with Premier League pedigree (Semenyo, Issahaku) capable of beating defenders in 1v1 situations
Experienced, disciplined midfield anchor in Thomas Partey providing defensive cover and ball distribution
Strong squad depth at forward positions and proven big-game captain in Jordan Ayew with 119 international caps
strengths_away: League-leading defensive organisation and pressing intensity from CONCACAF qualifying — Panama went unbeaten in their final 12 competitive qualifiers
Physicality and combative style well-suited to the pressures of a World Cup group-stage battle against fellow underdogs
Experienced leadership through record appearance-holder Anibal Godoy (157 caps) and proven goal-scorer Jose Fajardo (17 international goals)
